session.name: LTX-2.3 template: reinstated audio-latent subgraph, wired transition LoRA (cross-op), built input-audio voice lip-sync (WORKING via LTXVConditioning fps fix)

goal.original: Continue MPI-4: author the LTX-2.3 multi-mode template in live ComfyUI (cooperative session, user drives ComfyUI, agent edits workflow JSON directly). This session: pick next ops after i2v/t2v/FL.
goal.summary: Three features added to the live template this session, all working: (1) reinstated the stage-1 audio save/continue subgraph the prior session accidentally removed; (2) transition LoRA wired as a cross-op toggle (stage1+stage2, model-only, float-switch self-bypass); (3) INPUT AUDIO voice lip-sync built and CONFIRMED WORKING after adding the missing LTXVConditioning fps-embedding node. Open question for next session: input-audio influence is image-dependent (strength 1.0 worked on every image tested) -> may drop the slider entirely and just auto-enable at 1.0 when an audio file is present; needs more image/prompt testing to confirm.

completed: Reinstated stage-1 audio save/continue subgraph (9 nodes from .bak2) - fixed multi-stage audio break (CreateVideo 66.audio was unlinked; 124 Stage-1 Audio Latent was reading raw fresh not continue-aware)

Researched transition LoRA (valiantcat/LTX-2.3-Transition-LORA) - confirmed model-only (1152 tensors all diffusion_model.*, zero CLIP), works on i2v/t2v/FL not FL-only, downloaded to C:\AI\loras\LTX2.3\ltx2.3-transition.safetensors (372MB)

Wired transition LoRA: node 191 MpiLoraModel (model-only) + node 192 Input_Use_Transition (MpiBoolean float->strength self-bypass). User moved it to TRUNK (stage1+stage2) because stage-2-only had NO visual effect on live test
Investigated monolith input-audio section (saved research/monolith-input-audio.md) + BFS head-swap (saved research/monolith-bfs-headswap.md, DEFERRED)

Built input-audio chain in template: 197 LoadAudio(Input_Audio_File) -> 198 LTXVAudioVAEEncode -> 200 SetLatentNoiseMask <- 199 SolidMask <- 202 MpiNormalizeValue(inverse) <- 201 Input_Audio_Influence; gated by 204 MpiIfElse <- 203 Input_Use_Input_Audio; -> 145 stage-1 concat
User wired SolidMask 199 width/height to real latent dims (155/156 floor(a/2))

THE FIX: added node 212 LTXVConditioning 'LTX Conditioning (fps)' between conditioning reroutes 84->86, frame_rate <- 98 FPS <- 76 Duration.fps. This was the missing piece - input-audio lip-sync now WORKS
Added model DEPENDENCIES section to spec (7 files) + decision: extra LoRA ship as deps NOT user dropdown
Created Cubric-Audio app folder + README (voice-recording = future Cubric Audio capability, mic icon)

Test whether audio/video LENGTH must match (user ran 8s audio + 4s video, worked, audio captured at end - confirm if mismatch causes artifacts)
Then continue op build-out: video extend (latent-continue half-wired via IfElse 51/56, sampler-side feed unverified), then ControlNet, then head-swap (BFS - deferred, research saved)

next_action.description: Resume MPI-4 input-audio testing. User is testing whether input-audio strength 1.0 works on every image/prompt to decide if the Input_Audio_Influence slider can be dropped (auto-enable at 1.0 when audio file present). Re-scan the live template FIRST (G:\ComfyUi\ComfyUI\user\default\workflows\LTX_i2v_t2v_template.json) - prior context is summarized not live. After the slider decision, continue op build-out (video extend next).

key_decisions: Input-audio lip-sync FIX = LTXVConditioning fps embedding (node 212). Template had no LTXVConditioning so conditioning lacked fps metadata and audio timing never mapped to mouth motion. THIS was the missing piece - feature works now.

Input_Audio_Influence slider is INVERTED via MpiNormalizeValue mode=inverse: slider 1.0 = mask 0.0 = FULL audio strength (= monolith's mask-strength-0). Creator's video used mask 0.7 = our slider 0.3.
Influence is IMAGE-DEPENDENT: image A worked at slider 0.3 AND 1.0; image B needed 1.0 (0.3 = no movement). 1.0 = safe default. May drop slider entirely if 1.0 always works.
Transition LoRA on TRUNK (stage1+stage2), not stage-2-only - user's live test showed stage-2-only had NO visual effect, contradicting the official valiantcat workflow. Live test wins.
Extra LoRA (transition + gemma-abliterated) ship as model DEPENDENCIES, not user-dropdown entries.
constraints: COOPERATIVE LIVE SESSION: user drives ComfyUI; agent edits JSON on disk. ALWAYS ask user to CLOSE the ComfyUI tab AND confirm SAVED before writing JSON - disk != live until user saves. This bit us 3x this session (stale disk reads). Re-scan disk and CONFIRM saved-state before every write.

DO NOT TRUST SUB-AGENT CLAIMS without verifying against live JSON. A sub-agent FABRICATED audio_normalization_factors='1,1,0.33,1,1,0.33,1,1' and CFG=4 - both FALSE (real = all 1s, CFG=1). User caught it. Same skepticism as the LoRA author claims.
Windows Python cp1252 - ALWAYS run helpers with PYTHONUTF8=1, open files encoding='utf-8'.
MPI custom nodes only, never rgthree. MpiMath floor(a/2) not int(a/2).

Splice method that works: clone exact node shapes from existing instances (other templates/monolith), renumber link IDs past last_link_id, fix input.link + output.links refs, validate no dangling links before writing. Write a temp .py script, run, then rm it.
Node naming: Input_*/Output_* for app-read/write; Tier-1 titles bare (Seed, Duration, Lora_*, etc). Worker nodes get plain descriptive titles.
Monolith custom-audio chain was SAVED bypassed; user toggled ON and confirmed it WORKS = working reference exists. audio_normalization_factors = all 1s in both.

resume_prompt: You are resuming MPI-4 (LTX 2.3 video model integration) in a fresh session. Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/139e9611-9e1e-4f23-a503-25002c3ce3f4.json and use mpi-continue. COOPERATIVE LIVE session: the user drives ComfyUI on 127.0.0.1:8188 and you edit the workflow JSON on disk at G:\ComfyUi\ComfyUI\user\default\workflows\LTX_i2v_t2v_template.json. RE-SCAN that file FIRST (prior context is summarized, not live). This session built INPUT AUDIO voice lip-sync into the template and it WORKS - the fix was adding an LTXVConditioning node (212) that embeds fps into the conditioning. The open question the user is testing after lunch: does input-audio strength 1.0 work on EVERY image/prompt? If yes, drop the Input_Audio_Influence slider and just auto-enable input audio at strength 1.0 whenever an audio file is present (no slider, no button). Influence slider is INVERTED (slider 1.0 = mask 0 = full strength). After that decision, continue op build-out: video extend next, then ControlNet, then head-swap (BFS, deferred - research saved). CRITICAL GOTCHAS: (1) always confirm the user has SAVED and CLOSED the ComfyUI tab before writing JSON - disk != live until saved (bit us 3x). (2) DO NOT trust sub-agent research claims without verifying against live JSON - a sub-agent fabricated norm-factor and CFG values this session. (3) PYTHONUTF8=1 + utf-8 for all python helpers. (4) MPI nodes only, no rgthree. Full as-built details + decisions in research/ltx-integration-spec.md section 3d.
